summary: PR #87681 is the only hydrated open candidate and is a focused, useful contributor PR for the Linux exec SIGKILL/OOM-score diagnostic path. It is not merge-ready because checks were unavailable from the preflight artifact, mergeability is unknown, comments/review comments were not hydrated, and no Codex /review preflight is present. Plan a maintainer-safe repair/finalization pass on the contributor branch with preserved author credit.

actions.reason: Useful contributor PR should be finalized by repairing/refreshed validation rather than merged without proof or replaced without first trying the editable branch.
A complete executable repair artifact is available for the editable contributor branch; merge remains blocked until preflight proof is collected.

repair_candidate.summary: Repair and finalize contributor PR #87681, which adds Linux exec diagnostics for bare SIGKILL when the OOM-score wrapper is active. Keep the implementation focused on the existing four touched files, refresh against current main if needed, hydrate and address human/bot review comments, run Codex /review, and validate before any merge recommendation.
repair_candidate.pr_title: fix(exec): surface Linux OOM-score SIGKILL hint
repair_candidate.pr_body:
Clownfish repair/finalization path for #87681.

Source PR: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/87681
Canonical issue: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/69242
Credit: Thanks @sweetcornna for the original implementation and tests.

Plan:
- Refresh the contributor branch against current main if needed.
- Keep the fix scoped to exec runtime SIGKILL diagnostics and Linux OOM-score helper behavior.
- Hydrate and address human, Codex, Greptile, Asile, CodeRabbit, Copilot, and similar review-bot comments.
- Run Codex /review and address all findings.
- Validate with targeted vitest files and pnpm check:changed before merge consideration.
credit_notes: Preserve contributor author credit for @sweetcornna from source PR https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/87681.
If the executor must create a replacement PR instead of repairing the contributor branch, the replacement PR body must credit @sweetcornna and link the source PR as the origin of the fix.
